creator: konstantin.t.i@ya.ru
text:
Hello.
I suggest adding a check for the existence of the image when copying it to the user's target directory via "make install".

For what?
If I build only memstick + ftp, without cdrom
make memstick ftp

Then after executing the command
make install DESTDIR=/opt/iso/FreeBSD/RELEASE/14/14.2/
An error message will be issued stating that it is impossible to copy the non-existent disc1.iso file and other files.

Solution
In the file /usr/src/release/Makefile in the release-install section:
add a check for the existence of the copied image
.if exists(${I})


My fully working code is like this:
release-install:
.if defined(DESTDIR) && !empty(DESTDIR)
	mkdir -p ${DESTDIR}
.endif
	cp -a ftp ${DESTDIR}/
.if !empty(IMAGES)
.for I in ${IMAGES}
.if exists(${I})
	cp -p ${I} ${DESTDIR}/${OSRELEASE}-${I}
. if defined(WITH_COMPRESSED_IMAGES) && !empty(WITH_COMPRESSED_IMAGES)
	${XZ_CMD} -k ${DESTDIR}/${OSRELEASE}-${I}
. endif
. endif
.endfor
	( cd ${DESTDIR} && sha512 ${OSRELEASE}* > ${DESTDIR}/CHECKSUM.SHA512 )
	( cd ${DESTDIR} && sha256 ${OSRELEASE}* > ${DESTDIR}/CHECKSUM.SHA256 )
.endif


If there is already some solution that allows me to copy only the collected images I need, then please tell me how.
Thank you.
